title: Letzter–Morrison Problem on asymptotically optimal bounds for $n(\mathbb{Z}_p^d)$
statement_text: Prove an asymptotically optimal upper bound for $n(\mathbb{Z}_p^d)$, the smallest $n$ such that every $\mathbb{Z}_p^d$-labelling of the complete digraph on $n$ vertices contains a zero-sum directed cycle.
context_text: Letzter and Morrison showed that $n(\mathbb{Z}_p^d) \leq O(pd(\log d)^2)$ and $n(\mathbb{Z}_2^d) \leq O(d\log d)$, significantly improving the general bound $n(\Gamma)\leq|\Gamma|+1$ when $\Gamma$ is far from cyclic. They then posed the problem of obtaining an asymptotically optimal upper bound for $n(\mathbb{Z}_p^d)$.
paper_contribution: Proves improved upper bounds for $n(\mathbb{Z}_p^d)$, advancing toward the asymptotically optimal bound posed by Letzter and Morrison.
notes: The exact statement of the problem as posed by Letzter–Morrison is truncated in the provided abstract excerpt; the problem is clearly identified from context but its full formulation is not fully visible in the supplied content.
paper_title: Improved bounds for zero-sum cycles in $\mathbb{Z}_p^d$
